texts.full_text: racker. I don't have both oars in the water. I'm a bubble off plum. In other words, I'm crazier than a shithouse rat. These are my stories. Comments--short or long, nasty or nice--always welcome! Thursday, May 31, 2007 Passport Purgatory! Passport Originally uploaded by gravitywave With all the terrorist threats from within and without, it is somewhat ironic to me that the process of obtaining or renewing a passport is a journey to a special kind of bureaucratic purgatory, a place where the damned and the frustrated wander, trapped by the red tape of a system designed to protect, yet it seems, is more interested in testing the limits of the human spirit, or in my case, the limits of a bipolar writer's sanity, where the lines between reality and the abyss of madness are constantly blurred, and the quest for a simple travel document becomes a metaphor for the quest for a sense of identity and belonging in a chaotic, and, of course, a Naked City, where the sounds of the shithouse rats are the only constants in a world of madness and, in the words of the great philosopher, a world where the only true madness, is the madness of the shithouse rats, and the only true constants are the words, the

texts.full_text: , the father of three adult children and husband to Gretta for 40 years disappears. The novel follows their lives over five days in London during a heatwave that mirrors rising tensions within this Irish clan who are now forced into an uncertain future without him by his sudden absence In his absence, which stretches to one hour, then two, then 10, Gretta recalls how Robert always “likes to know where she is at all times, he frets if she leaves the house without telling him, gets edgy if she slips away without him seeing, and starts ringing the children to question them on her whereabouts.” It used to drive her crazy, when they were first married, she sa

texts.full_text: Monday in the journal JAMA Internal Medicine said acupuncture may be helpful in reducing the frequency of migraines and preventing The post The Long-term Effect of Acupuncture ... 6 Acupuncture CThe Long-term Effect of Acupuncture for Migraine Prophylaxis, A Randomized Clinical Trial A study published in the journal JAMA Internal Medicine has found that acupuncture may be helpful in reducing the frequency of migraines and in preventing migraines in the long-term. The study, which involved a randomized clinical trial, compared the effects of acupuncture, a acupuncture, and a waiting list control. The results showed that the acupuncture group experienced a significant reduction in the frequency of migraines compared to the control and sham acupuncture groups. The effects of acupuncture were also noted to be with the reduction in migraines persisting for a significant period of time after the treatment period. 6 Ways Acupuncture Can Improve Your Health and Well-being 1. Pain Relief: Acupuncture is widely recognized for its effectiveness in treating a variety

texts.full_text: versy related to the proper diagnosis of LPR. Up till now, there is no ideal test that can be considered for LPR detection. Many methods are used in both adults and children for LPR diagnosis including pH monitoring, upper gastro-intestinal endoscopy, esophageal manometry, esophageal biopsy, and intramural impedance. However, the results of these tests are not reproducible in patients with pediatric LPR. Dual probe 24-hour pH monitoring is thought for many years to be the gold standard test for diagnosing LPR. It is, however, an invasive test of low sensitivity, with an incidence of false-negative results reaching as high as 50% \[[@b8-ceo-2015-00409]-[@b11-ceo-2015-00409]\]. The Dx-pH measurement system (Dx-pH, Respiratory Technology Corp., San Diego, CA, USA) is a minimally invasive and relatively new device that has been reported to be sensitive in detection of acidic reflux events in the posterior oropharynx \[[@b12-ceo-2015-00409]\]. The device uses a thin transnasal catheter (probe) that ends with a pH sensor capable of detection both liquid and aerosolized form of acid. The probe can be easily introduced in the nose and attached to a wireless transmitter at its external end. The tip of the probe has the shape of a tear drop with a flashing red light that helps in determining the proper placement of the catheter in the oropharynx and contains the pH sensor \[[@b12-ceo-2015-00409]\]. In the few studies that included oropharyngeal pH monitoring in children, insertion of the probe was performed under general anesthesia \[[@b13-ceo-2015-00409]\]. The aims of this study were to explore the possibility of conducting and inserting oropharyngeal pH probe for children in the outpatient clinic setup and to explore the results of this test in
